sites_https_justification: All project URLs (GitHub repository, npm package) are HTTPS-only.
contribution_justification: Uses GitHub issues and pull requests; CONTRIBUTING.md documents how to contribute: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md
floss_license_justification: The MIT license is approved by the Open Source Initiative (OSI).
floss_license_osi_justification: The MIT license is approved by the Open Source Initiative (OSI).
documentation_basics_justification: The README documents installation, configuration, usage, and output: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune#readme
documentation_interface_justification: The external interface is the CLI (`gqlprune` and `gqlprune init`) and the gqlPrune.config.yaml schema (graphqlDir, srcDir, excludedFolders, usagePatterns), all documented in the README: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune#configuration
repo_track_justification: Repository on GitHub using git, which tracks every change, its author, and timestamp.
repo_interim_justification: Development happens in the open via commits and pull requests; interim versions between releases are public: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/commits/main
repo_distributed_justification: Repository uses git, a distributed version control system.
version_unique_justification: Each user-facing release has a unique semantic version, published to npm (https://www.npmjs.com/package/gqlprune) and tagged in git (e.g. v2.0.1).
version_semver_justification: Releases follow Semantic Versioning, managed by release-please.
version_tags_justification: Every release is tagged in git with a matching GitHub Release: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/releases
release_notes_justification: Release notes are maintained in CHANGELOG.md (generated by release-please from Conventional Commits): https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/blob/main/CHANGELOG.md
release_notes_vulns_justification: No publicly known vulnerabilities have been fixed in a release to date; when one is, it will be noted in the changelog.
report_tracker_justification: The project uses GitHub Issues as its bug tracker: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/issues
report_process_justification: Bugs are reported via GitHub Issues; security issues via SECURITY.md: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/blob/main/SECURITY.md
report_responses_justification: Reports are triaged and responded to via GitHub Issues; volume is low and received reports have been addressed.
enhancement_responses_justification: Enhancement requests are accepted and responded to via GitHub Issues.
report_archive_justification: GitHub Issues provides a publicly available, searchable archive: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/issues?q=is%3Aissue
vulnerability_report_process_justificati: The vulnerability-reporting process is published in SECURITY.md: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/blob/main/SECURITY.md
vulnerability_report_private_justificati: Private reports are supported via GitHub's private vulnerability reporting (Security Advisories): https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/security/advisories/new
vulnerability_report_response_justificat: SECURITY.md commits to an initial acknowledgement within 5 business days (well under the 14-day requirement). No vulnerability reports have been received to date.
build_justification: The project builds from source with `npm run build` (the TypeScript compiler).
build_common_tools_justification: Built with common, widely available tools: npm and tsc (TypeScript).
build_floss_tools_justification: Built entirely with FLOSS tools (Node.js, npm, TypeScript).
test_justification: Automated Jest test suite covering the utilities, core pruner, config generator, and CLI dispatch.
test_invocation_justification: Tests run with a single command: `npm test`.
test_most_justification: Major functionality is covered; ~97% statement coverage, enforced by a coverage threshold in CI.
test_policy_justification: Policy: new functionality and fixes must include tests. https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#testing-policy
tests_are_added_justification: New features and fixes ship with tests, visible in the commit/PR history.
tests_documented_added_justification: The testing policy is documented in CONTRIBUTING.md: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#testing-policy
warnings_justification: TypeScript strict mode, `tsc --noEmit`, and ESLint run in CI; the build fails on errors.
warnings_fixed_justification: CI fails on type and lint errors, so they are fixed before merge.
warnings_strict_justification: tsconfig.json sets strict: true (maximally strict TypeScript).
know_secure_design_justification: The maintainer applies secure-design principles: least privilege (scoped CI tokens / OIDC), input validation, and safe defaults. The tool reads files read-only and executes no project code.
know_common_errors_justification: The maintainer is familiar with common vulnerability classes (injection, path traversal); the tool only reads files and matches strings, running no user-supplied code and making no network calls.
delivery_mitm_justification: Distribution channels use HTTPS exclusively (npm, GitHub), and npm releases are published with provenance attestations via Trusted Publishing (OIDC).
delivery_unsigned_justification: All downloads are over HTTPS (npm/GitHub); no HTTP delivery. Releases carry npm provenance attestations.
vulnerabilities_fixed_60_days_justificat: Dependencies are monitored via Dependabot, Socket, and OpenSSF Scorecard; vulnerabilities are fixed within 60 days.
vulnerabilities_critical_fixed_justifica: Critical vulnerabilities are prioritized and fixed promptly.
static_analysis_justification: GitHub CodeQL code scanning runs on pushes, pull requests, and on a schedule; ESLint (typescript-eslint) and strict `tsc --noEmit` also run in CI.
static_analysis_common_vulnerabilities_j: CodeQL's default security query suite checks for common vulnerability patterns.
static_analysis_fixed_justification: CodeQL findings surface in GitHub code scanning and are addressed.
static_analysis_often_justification: Static analysis runs on every push and pull request, not only at release.
dynamic_analysis_justification: The Jest suite executes the real code paths (config parsing, file discovery, usage detection, CLI dispatch) against fixtures on every CI run.
dynamic_analysis_enable_assertions_justi: The Jest suite runs extensive assertions during testing/CI; these run only during testing, not in the published build (only dist/ ships).
dynamic_analysis_fixed_justification: No medium-or-higher severity exploitable vulnerabilities have been found through testing; any finding would be fixed promptly per the project's 60-day policy and SECURITY.md.
test_continuous_integration_justificatio: CI runs build, lint, and tests on every push and pull request: https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/blob/main/.github/workflows/ci.yml
discussion_justification: GitHub supports discussion on issues and pull requests.
no_leaked_credentials_justification: No credentials are stored in the repository; CI uses GitHub Actions secrets and OIDC.
english_justification: All documentation (README, CONTRIBUTING.md, SECURITY.md) and code comments are in English; issues, pull requests, and code review are conducted in English.
maintained_justification: Actively maintained: regular commits and automated releases via release-please (currently on the 2.x line), CI on every push/PR, dependencies monitored via Dependabot + Socket + OpenSSF Scorecard, and issues triaged on GitHub. https://github.com/Krister-Johansson/gqlPrune/releases
